author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm’s lintel is thus inscribed: “Unto the end, for Idithun, a Psalm to Asaph himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “Unto the end” is, ye know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Idithun is interpreted “leaping over those men,” Asaph is interpreted “a congregation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here therefore there is speaking “a congregation that leapeth over,” in order that it may reach the End, which is Christ Jesus.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my voice,” he saith, “to the Lord I have cried” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But many men cry unto the Lord for the sake of getting riches and avoiding losses, for the safety of their friends, for the security of their house, for temporal felicity, for secular dignity, lastly, even for mere soundness of body, which is the inheritance of the poor man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For such and such like things many men do cry unto the Lord; scarce one for the sake of the Lord Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For an easy thing it is for a man to desire anything of the Lord, and not to desire the Lord Himself; as if forsooth that which He giveth could be sweeter than Himself that giveth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whosoever therefore doth cry unto the Lord for the sake of any other thing, is not yet one that leapeth over.…He doth indeed hearken to thee at the time when thou dost seek Himself, not when through Himself thou dost seek any other thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath been said of some men, “They cried, and there was no one to save them; to the Lord, and He hearkened not unto them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the voice of them was not unto the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This the Scripture doth express in another place, where it saith of such men, “On the Lord they have not called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto Him they have not ceased to cry, and yet upon the Lord they have not called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, upon the Lord they have not called?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have not called the Lord unto themselves: they have not invited the Lord to their heart, they would not have themselves inhabited by the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore what hath befallen them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They have trembled with fear where fear was not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have trembled about the loss of things present, for the reason that they were not full of Him, upon whom they have not called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have not loved gratis, so that after the loss of temporal things they could say, “As it hath pleased the Lord, so hath been done, be the name of the Lord blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore this man saith, “My voice is unto the Lord, and He doth hearken unto me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him show us how this cometh to pass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the day of tribulation I have sought out God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who art thou that doest this thing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the day of thy tribulation take heed what thou seekest out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If a jail be the cause of tribulation, thou seekest to get forth from jail: if fever be the cause of tribulation, thou seekest health: if hunger be the cause of tribulation, thou seekest fulness: if losses be the cause of tribulation, thou seekest gain: if expatriation be the cause of tribulation, thou seekest the home of thy flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why should I name all things, or when could I name all things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to be one leaping over?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the day of thy tribulation seek out God: not through God some other thing, but out of tribulation God, that to this end God may take away tribulation, that thou mayest without anxiety cleave unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the day of my tribulation, I have sought out God:” not any other thing, but “God I have sought out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how hast thou sought out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my hands in the night before Him.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Tribulation must not be thought to be this or that in particular.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For every individual that doth not yet leap over, thinketh that as yet to be no tribulation, unless it be a thing which may have befallen this life of some sad occasion: but this man, that leapeth over, doth count this whole life to be his tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so much doth he love his supernal country, that the earthly pilgrimage is of itself the greatest tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how can this life be otherwise than a tribulation, I pray you? how can that not be a tribulation, the whole whereof hath been called temptation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast it written in the book of Job, is not human life a temptation upon earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath he said, human life is tempted upon earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, but life itself is a temptation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore temptation, it must surely be a tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this tribulation therefore, that is to say in this life, this man that leapeth over hath sought out God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my hands,” he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “with my hands”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With my works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he was not seeking any thing corporeal, so that he might find and handle something which he had lost, so that he might seek with hands coin, gold, silver, vesture, in short everything which can be held in the hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Howbeit, even our Lord Jesus Christ Himself willed Himself to be sought after with hands, when to His doubting disciple He showed the scars.…What then, to us belongeth not the seeking with hands?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It belongeth to us, as I have said, to seek with works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the night.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in the night”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this age.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is night until there shine forth day in the glorified advent of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For would ye see how it is night?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unless we had here had a lantern, we should have remained in darkness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Peter saith,“ We too have more sure the prophetic discourse, whereunto ye do well to give heed, as to a lantern shining in a dark place, until day shine, and the day-star arise in your hearts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is therefore to come day after this night, meanwhile in this night a lantern is not lacking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is perchance what we are now doing: by explaining these passages, we are bringing in a lantern, in order that we may rejoice in this night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which indeed ought alway to be burning in your houses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to such men is said, “The Spirit quench ye not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as though explaining what he was saying, he continueth and saith, “Prophecy despise ye not:” that is, let the lantern alway shine in you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And even this light by comparison with a sort of ineffable day is called night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the very life of believers by comparison with the life of unbelievers is day.…Night and day—day in comparison with unbelievers, night in comparison with the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Angels have a day, which we have not yet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already we have one that unbelievers have not: but not yet have believers that which Angels have: but they will have, at the time when they will be equal to the Angels of God, that which hath been promised to them in the Resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this then which is now day and yet night; night in comparison with the future day for which we yearn, day in comparison with the past night which we have renounced: in this night then, I say, let us seek God with our hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not works cease, let us seek God, be there no idle yearning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we are in the way, let us expend our means in order that we may be able to reach the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With hands let us seek God.…“With my hands in the night before Him, and I have not been deceived.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5. …“My soul hath refused to be comforted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So great weariness did here possess me, that my soul did close the door against all comfort.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence such weariness to him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It may be that his vineyard hath been hailed on, or his olive hath yielded no fruit, or the vintage hath been interrupted by rain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the weariness to him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear this out of another Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For therein is the voice of the same: “weariness hath bowed me down, because of sinners forsaking Thy law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith then that he was overcome with so great weariness because of this sort of evil thing; so as that his soul refused to be comforted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Weariness had well nigh swallowed him up, and sorrow had ingulfed him altogether beyond remedy, he refuseth to be comforted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then remained?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the first place, see whence he is comforted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had he not waited for one who might condole with him?…“I have been mindful of God, and I have been delighted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My hands had not wrought in vain, they had found a great comforter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While not being idle, “I have been mindful of God, and I have been delighted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God must therefore be praised, of whom this man being mindful, hath been delighted, and hath been comforted in sorrowful case, and refreshed when safety was in a manner despaired of: God must therefore be praised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fine, because he hath been comforted, in continuation he saith, “I have babbled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that same comfort being made mindful of God, I have been delighted, and have “babbled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “I have babbled”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have rejoiced, I have exulted in speaking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For babblers they are properly called, that by the common people are named talkative, who at the approach of joy are neither able nor willing to be silent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man hath become such an one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And again he saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my spirit hath fainted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With weariness he had pined away; by calling to mind God, he had been delighted, again in babbling he had fainted: what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All mine enemies have anticipated watches” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All mine enemies have kept watch over me; they have exceeded in keeping watch over me; in watching they have been beforehand with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where do they not lay traps?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have not mine enemies anticipated all watches?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who are these enemies, but they of whom the Apostle saith, “Ye have not wrestling against flesh and blood.”…Against the devil and his angels we are waging hostilities.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rulers of the world he hath called them, because they do themselves rule the lovers of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they do not rule the world, as if they were rulers of heaven and earth: but he is calling sinners the world.…With the devil and his angels there is no concord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They do themselves grudge us the kingdom of Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They cannot at all be appeased towards us: because “all mine enemies have anticipated watches.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have watched more to deceive than I to guard myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how can they have done otherwise than anticipate watches, that have set everywhere scandals, everywhere traps?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Weariness doth invest the heart, we have to fear lest sorrow swallow us up: in joy to fear lest the spirit faint in babbling: “all mine enemies have anticipated watches.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fine, in the midst of that same babbling, whiles thou art speaking, and art speaking without fear, how much is oft-times found which enemies would lay hold of and censure, whereon they would even found accusation and slander—“he said so, he thought so, he spake so!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What should man do, save that which followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been troubled, and I spake not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when he was troubled, lest in his babbling enemies anticipating watches should seek and find slanders, he spake not.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have thought on ancient days” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now he, as if he were one who had been beaten out of doors, hath taken refuge within: he is conversing in the secret place of his own heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And let him declare to us what he is doing there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is well with him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe what things he is thinking of, I pray you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is within, in his own house he is thinking of ancient days.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one saith to him, thou hast spoken ill: no one saith to him, thou hast spoken much: no one saith to him, thou hast thought perversely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus may it be well with him, may God aid him: let him think of the ancient days, and let him tell us what he hath done in his very inner chamber, whereunto he hath arrived, over what he hath leaped, where he hath abode.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have thought on ancient days; and of eternal years I have been mindful.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are eternal years?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a mighty thought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See whether this thought requireth anything but great silence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Apart from all noise without, from all tumult of things human let him remain quiet within, that would think of those eternal years.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are the years wherein we are eternal, or those wherein our ancestors have been, or those wherein our posterity are to be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far be it that they should be esteemed eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what part of these years doth remain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold we speak and say, “in this year:” and what have we got of this year, save the one day wherein we are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the former days of this year have already gone by, and are not to be had; but the future days have not yet come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In one day we are, and we say, in this year: nay rather say thou, to-day, if thou desirest to speak of anything present.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of the whole year what hast thou got that is present?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatsoever thereof is past, is no longer; whatsoever thereof is future, is not yet: how then, “this year”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Amend the expression: say, to-day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou speakest truth, henceforth I will say, “to-day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again observe this too, how to-day the morning hours have already past, the future hours have not yet come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This too therefore amend: say, in this hour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And of this hour what hast thou got?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some moments thereof have already gone by, those that are future have not yet come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say, in this moment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what moment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While I am uttering syllables, if I shall speak two syllables, the latter doth not sound until the former hath gone by: in a word, in that same one syllable, if it chance to have two letters, the latter letter doth not sound, until the former hath gone by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then have we got of these years?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These years are changeable: the eternal years must be thought on, years that stand, that are not made up of days that come and depart; years whereof in another place the Scripture saith to God, “But Thou art the Self-same, and Thy years shall not fail.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On these years this man that leapeth over, not in babbling without, but in silence hath thought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I have meditated in the night with my heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No slanderous person seeketh for snares in his words, in his heart he hath meditated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I babbled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold there is the former babbling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Watch again, that thy spirit faint not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I did not, he saith, I did not so babble as if it were abroad: in another way now.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I did babble, and did search out my spirit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he were searching the earth to find veins of gold, no one would say that he was foolish; nay, many men would call him wise, for desiring to come at gold: how great treasures hath a man within, and he diggeth not!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man was examining his spirit, and was speaking with that same his spirit, and in the very speaking he was babbling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was questioning himself, was examining himself, was judge over himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he continueth; “I did search my spirit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He had to fear lest he should stay within his own spirit: for he had babbled without; and because all his enemies had anticipated watches, he found there sorrow, and his spirit fainted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that did babble without, lo, now doth begin to babble within in safety, where being alone in secret, he is thinking on eternal years.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thou hast found what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God will not repel for everlasting” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Weariness he had found in this life; in no place a trustworthy, in no place a fearless comfort.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto whatsoever men he betook himself, in them he found scandal, or feared it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In no place therefore was he free from care.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An evil thing it was for him to hold his peace, lest perchance he should keep silence from good words; to speak and babble without was painful to him, lest all his enemies, anticipating watches, should seek slanders in his words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Being exceedingly straitened in this life, he thought much of another life, where there is not this trial.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when is he to arrive thither?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it cannot but be evident that our suffering here is the anger of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thing is spoken of in Isaiah, “I will not be an avenger unto you for everlasting, nor will I be angry with you at all times.” …Will this anger of God always abide?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This man hath not found this in silence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God will not repel for everlasting, and He will not add any more that it should be well-pleasing to Him still.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, that it should be well-pleasing to Him still to repel, and He will not add the repelling for everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He must needs recall to Himself His servants, He must needs receive fugitives returning to the Lord, He must needs hearken to the voice of them that are in fetters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Or unto the end will He cut off mercy from generation to generation?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Or will God forget to be merciful?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In thee, from thee unto another there is no mercy unless God bestow it on thee: and shall God Himself forget mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The stream runneth: shall the spring itself be dried up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Or shall God forget to be merciful: or shall He keep back in anger His mercies?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, shall He be so angry, as that He will not have mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will more easily keep back anger than mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I said.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now leaping over himself he hath said what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Now I have begun:” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10), when I had gone out even from myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here henceforth there is no danger: for even to remain in myself, was danger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I said, Now I have begun: this is the changing of the right hand of the Lofty One.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the Lofty One hath begun to change me: now I have begun something wherein I am secure: now I have entered a certain palace of joys, wherein no enemy is to be feared: now I have begun to be in that region, where all mine enemies do not anticipate watches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Now I have begun: this is the changing of the right hand of the Lofty One.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been mindful of the works of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now behold him roaming among the works of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he was babbling without, and being made sorrowful thereby his spirit fainted: he babbled within with his own heart, and with his spirit, and having searched out that same spirit he was mindful of the eternal years, was mindful of the mercy of the Lord, how God will not repel him for everlasting; and he began now fearlessly to rejoice in His works, fearlessly to exult in the same.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear now those very works, and let us too exult.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let even us leap over in our affections, and not rejoice in things temporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we too have our bed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do we not enter therein?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do we not abide in silence?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do we not search out our spirit?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do we not think on the eternal years?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do we not rejoice in the works of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such sort now let us hear, and let us take delight in Himself speaking, in order that when we shall have departed hence, we may do that which we used to do while He spake; if only we are making the beginning of Him whereof he spake in, “Now I have begun.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To rejoice in the works of God, is to forget even thyself, if thou canst delight in Him alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is a better thing than He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou not see that, when thou returnest to thyself, thou returnest to a worse thing? “for I shall be mindful from the beginning of Thy wonderful works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will meditate on all Thy works, and on Thy affections I will babble” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the third babbling!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He babbled without, when he hinted; he babbled in his spirit within, when he advanced: he babbled on the works of God, when he arrived at the place toward which he advanced.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And on Thy affections:” not on any affections.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What man doth live without affections?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And do ye suppose, brethren, that they who fear God, worship God, love God, have not any affections?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou indeed suppose and dare to suppose, that painting, the theatre, hunting, hawking, fishing, engage the affections, and the meditation on God doth not engage certain interior affections of its own, while we contemplate the universe, and place before our eyes the spectacle of the natural world, and therein labour to discover the Maker, and find Him nowhere unpleasing, but pleasing above all things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, Thy way is in the Holy One” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is contemplating now the works of the mercy of God around us, out of these he is babbling, and in these affections he is exulting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At first he is beginning from thence, “Thy way is in the Holy One?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that way of Thine which is in the Holy One?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am,” He saith, “the Way, the Truth, and the Life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Return therefore, ye men, from your affections.…“Who is a great God, like our God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Gentiles have their affections regarding their gods, they adore idols, they have eyes and they see not; ears they have and they hear not; feet they have and they walk not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why dost thou walk to a God that walketh not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I do not, he saith, worship such things, and what dost thou worship?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The divinity which is there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost then worship that whereof hath been said elsewhere, “for the Gods of the nations are demons.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost either worship idols, or devils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neither idols, nor devils, he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what dost thou worship?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The stars, sun, moon, those things celestial.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much better Him that hath made both things earthly and things celestial.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who is a great God like our God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art the God that doest wonderful things alone” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art indeed a great God, doing wonderful things in body, in soul; alone doing them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The deaf have heard, the blind have seen, the feeble have recovered, the dead have risen, the paralytic have been strengthened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But these miracles were at that time performed on bodies, let us see those wrought on the soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sober are those that were a little before drunken, believers are those that were a little before worshippers of idols: their goods they bestow on the poor that did rob before those of others.…“Wonderful things alone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moses too did them, but not alone: Elias too did them, even Eliseus did them, the Apostles too did them, but no one of them alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That they might have power to do them, Thou wast with them: when Thou didst them they were not with Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were not with Thee when Thou didst them, inasmuch as Thou didst make even these very men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How “alone”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it perchance the Father, and not the Son?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or the Son, and not the Father?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, but Father and Son and Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not three Gods but one God that doeth wonderful things alone, and even in this very leaper-over.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even his leaping over and arriving at these things was a miracle of God: when he was babbling within with his own spirit, in order that he might leap over even that same spirit of his, and might delight in the works of God, he then did wonderful things himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God hath done what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast made known unto the people Thy power.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence this congregation of Asaph leaping over; because He hath made known in the peoples His virtue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What virtue of His hath He made known in the peoples?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But we preach Christ crucified,…Christ the power of God and the wisdom of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then the virtue of God is Christ, He hath made known Christ in the peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we not yet perceive so much as this; and are we so unwise, are we lying so much below, do we so leap over nothing, as that we see not this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast redeemed in Thine arm Thy people” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With Thine arm,” that is, with Thy power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast redeemed in Thine arm Thy people, the sons of Israel and of Joseph.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How as if two peoples, “the sons of Israel and of Joseph”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not the sons of Joseph among the sons of Israel?…He hath admonished us of some distinction to be made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us search out our spirit, perchance God hath placed there something—God whom we ought even by night to seek with our hands, in order that we may not be deceived—perchance we shall discover even ourselves in this distinction of “sons of Israel and of Joseph.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By Joseph He hath willed another people to be understood, hath willed that the people of the Gentiles be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why the people of the Gentiles by Joseph?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Joseph was sold into Egypt by his brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Joseph whom the brethren envied, and sold him into Egypt, when sold into Egypt, toiled, was humbled; when made known and exalted, flourished, reigned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And by all these things he hath signified what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What but Christ sold by His brethren, banished from His own land, as it were into the Egypt of the Gentiles?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There at first humbled, when the Martyrs were suffering persecutions: now exalted, as we see; inasmuch as there hath been fulfilled in Him, “There shall adore Him all kinds of the earth, all nations shall serve Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore Joseph is the people of the Gentiles, but Israel the people of the Hebrew nation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath redeemed His people, “the sons of Israel and of Joseph.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By means of what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By means of the corner stone, wherein the two walls have been joined together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he continueth how?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The waters have seen Thee, O God, and they have feared and the abysses have been troubled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the waters?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are these waters hath been asked in the Apocalypse, the answer was, the peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There we find most clearly waters put by a figure for peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But above he had said, “Thou hast made known in the peoples Thy virtue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason therefore, “the waters have seen Thee, and they have feared.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have been changed because they have feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the abysses?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The depths of waters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What man among the peoples is not troubled, when the conscience is smitten?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou seekest the depth of the sea, what is deeper than human conscience?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is the depth which was troubled, when God redeemed with His arm His people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what manner were the abysses troubled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When all men poured forth their consciences in confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In praises of God, in confessions of sins, in hymns and in songs, in prayers, “There is a multitude of the sound of waters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The clouds have uttered a voice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence that sound of waters, thence the troubling of the abysses, because “the clouds have uttered a voice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What clouds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The preachers of the word of truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What clouds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those concerning which God doth menace a certain vineyard, which instead of grape had brought forth thorns and He saith, “I will command My clouds, that they rain no rain upon it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a word, the Apostles forsaking the Jews, went to the Gentiles: in preaching Christ among all nations, “the clouds have uttered a voice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thine arrows have gone through.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those same voices of the clouds He hath again called arrows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the words of the Evangelists were arrows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these things are allegories.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For properly neither an arrow is rain, nor rain is an arrow: but yet the word of God is both an arrow because it doth smite; and rain because it doth water.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one therefore any longer wonder at the troubling of the abysses, when “Thine arrows have gone through.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “have gone through”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have not stopped in the ears, but they have pierced the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The voice of Thy thunder is in the wheel” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How are we to understand it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May the Lord give aid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When boys we were wont to imagine, whenever we heard thunderings from Heaven, that carriages were going forth as it were from the stables.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thunder doth make a sort of rolling like carriages.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Must we return to these boyish thoughts, in order to understand, “the voice of Thy thunder is in the wheel,” as though God hath certain carriages in the clouds, and the passing along of the carriages doth raise that sound?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is boyish, vain, trifling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is then, “The voice of Thy thunder is in the wheel”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy voice rolleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not even this do I understand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall we do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us question Idithun himself, to see whether perchance he may himself explain what he hath said: “The voice,” he saith, “of Thy thunder is in the wheel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I do not understand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will hear what thou sayest: “Thy lightnings have appeared to the round world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say then, I had no understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The round world is a wheel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the circuit of the round world is with reason called also an “orb:” whence also a small wheel is called an “orbiculus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The voice of Thy thunder is in the wheel:” Thy “lightnings have appeared to the round world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those clouds in a wheel have gone about the round world, have gone about with thundering and with lightning, they have shaken the abyss, with commandments they have thundered, with miracles they have lightened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto every land hath gone forth the sound of them, and unto the ends of the orb the words of them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The land hath been moved and made to tremble:” that is, all men that dwell in the land.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by a figure the land itself is sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because all nations are called by the name of sea, inasmuch as human life is bitter, and exposed to storms and tempests.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Moreover if thou observe this, how men devour one another like fishes, how the stronger doth swallow up the weaker—it is then a sea, unto it the Evangelists went.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy way is in the sea” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now Thy way was in the Holy One, now “Thy way is in the sea:” because the Holy One Himself is in the sea, and with reason even did walk upon the waters of the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy way is in the sea,” that is, Thy Christ is preached among the Gentiles.…“Thy way is in the sea, and Thy paths in many waters,” that is, in many peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy footsteps will not be known.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath touched certain, and wonder were it if it be not those same Jews.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold now the mercy of Christ hath been so published to the Gentiles, that “Thy way is in the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy footsteps will not be known.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How so, by whom will they not be known, save by those who still say, Christ hath not yet come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do they say, Christ hath not yet come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they do not yet recognise Him walking on the sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast led home Thy people like sheep in the hand of Moses and of Aaron” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why He hath added this is somewhat difficult to discover.…They banished Christ; sick as they were, they would not have Him for their Saviour; but He began to be among the Gentiles, and among all nations, among many peoples.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, a remnant of that people hath been saved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The ungrateful multitude hath remained without, even the halting breadth of Jacob’s thigh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the breadth of the thigh is understood of the multitude of lineage, and among the greater part of the Israelites a certain multitude became vain and foolish, so as not to know the steps of Christ on the waters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast led home Thy people like sheep,” and they have not known Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though Thou hast done such great benefits unto them, hast divided sea, hast made them pass over dry land between waters, hast drowned in the waves pursuing enemies, in the desert hast rained manna for their hunger, leading them home “by the hand of Moses and Aaron:” still they thrust Thee from them, so that in the sea was Thy Way, and Thy steps they knew not.
